predicate.title: Wire usage evidence into the standard CR workflow
predicate.problem: roadmap-priority-2-metrics-integrity's exit criterion for finding 1 requires that every merged Change Record after the usage.py transcript-path fix (CR-20260715-005) carries usage evidence, but usage.py is a manual CLI script never invoked by new-change.py, any git hook, or the local self-check — 13 of 13 changes since the fix still have no evidence/usage.json, and the dashboard shows n/a for all of them
acceptance_criteria: new-change.py's scaffold output prints the exact, copy-pasteable usage.py invocation for the newly-minted change (change-id, opened-at, evidence dir), so recording usage evidence is a one-command nudge rather than something to remember from scratch

spec/tools/check.py, the standard local pre-PR self-check, prints a non-blocking reminder listing any already-committed change whose evidence/intent.json has a real (non-placeholder) gitCommit but no evidence/usage.json yet
the reminder is skipped for CI-authored changes (no local transcript to summarize) and for changes still on the placeholder gitCommit (too early to have a real commit to summarize)
out_of_scope: hard-blocking merges on missing usage evidence via a new G4 catalogue control -- rejected as contrary to the documented self-reported, gracefully-degrading design in metric-token-usage.yaml, and as disproportionate blast-radius for a metrics-quality nudge
